risk_of_bias.support_text: Summary: The article states that this was a “prospective randomized controlled trial (RCT)” and that patients “were randomized to the control group” and “randomized to the intervention group,” but it does not describe the actual random sequence generation method (e.g., computer-generated list, random numbers, blocks, stratification). Comment: Because “randomized” is given only in general terms without a specific randomization procedure, this is insufficient to confirm a truly random allocation sequence, but there is also no evidence of a non-random method.

Summary: The article states that the study was a randomized controlled trial and that 57 patients were assigned to control and 53 to intervention, but it gives no description of how the upcoming assignment was concealed from the recruiter. The extracted evidence explicitly says concealment was "not described" and no concealment mechanism (e.g., central allocation, SNOSE, or identical containers) is reported. Comment: Randomization alone does not establish allocation concealment, so without an explicit concealment method the risk remains unclear.

_evidence.note: The article states only that patients were randomized (57 control, 53 intervention) but gives no description of how the upcoming assignment was concealed from the recruiter.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Summary: The article describes a randomized trial of MBSR versus control and reports subjective outcomes (depression PHQ-9, stress scale, SF-12 mental/physical health) as well as objective outcomes, but it does not state whether participants or intervention personnel were blinded. Comment: Because blinding of participants/personnel is not described, and the extracted evidence says the blinding method is "not described" with participants/personnel "unclear," the domain cannot be judged Low; however, absence of blinding is not explicitly stated, so High is not justified.

Quote: "Albuminuria was determined using 24-h urine on 3 consecutive days. All routine blood parameters were analyzed in the Clinical Laboratory of the University of Heidelberg using standardized and certified methods; blood pressure was examined with a 24-h measurement. Psychiatric comorbidity and levels of depression and stress were assessed using the Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ). Subjective health status was measured by the 12-item short-form health survey (SF-12)" Comment: The outcome assessment included a mix of objective measures and patient-reported subjective outcomes. Because depression, stress, and SF-12 health status were assessed by self-report, the patient was effectively the assessor for those outcomes. The text does not state that patients were blinded, and lack of blinding could clearly influence these subjective measurements. Under the worst-outcome rule, the presence of these unblinded subjective outcomes makes the domain High risk even though some outcomes were objective.

outcomes.label: subjective health status (SF-12 mental and physical component summary)

risk_of_bias.support_text: Summary: The article reports that 57 patients were randomized to control and 53 to intervention, and that "nine patients in the intervention group did not attend the training sessions as required (less than five sessions; for reasons, see Supplementary Fig. 1)," with an intent-to-treat analysis and multiple imputation sensitivity analysis. However, the supplied text does not give analysed denominators for the outcome time point or any control-arm loss, and the nine missed sessions are explicitly tied to per-protocol participation rather than clearly documented missing outcome data. Therefore, the available evidence is insufficient to determine whether attrition on the analysed outcomes was high or imbalanced.

per_arm_flow.reasons: "nine patients in the intervention group did not attend the training sessions as required (less than five sessions; for reasons, see Supplementary Fig. 1)"

_evidence.missing_data_method: intent-to-treat analysis; sensitivity analyses with multiple imputation; per-protocol analysis
_evidence.notes: The only explicit post-randomisation attrition mentioned is 9 intervention participants not attending enough training sessions for the per-protocol analysis; no analysed n or control-arm loss is reported in the text provided.
